title: Zero-Click Attacks: AI Agents as Weapons

modules.title: What Zero-Click Means and Why AI Agents Raise the Stakes
modules.sub: Defining a threat class that needs no victim interaction
modules.description: Learners examine the defining characteristics of zero-click attacks and contrast them with traditional user-triggered exploits. They analyze how granting AI agents persistent access to communication and data systems fundamentally expands the zero-click attack surface, building a threat model that frames every subsequent module.

modules.title: Attack Vectors: Email Parsing, Calendar Invites, and Agentic Pipelines
modules.sub: Mapping the input channels attackers exploit against autonomous agents
modules.description: Learners trace how malicious payloads can enter agentic systems through routine inputs such as email messages, calendar events, and API responses. They map each vector to the agent permissions and downstream actions that make it dangerous, producing a vector-risk matrix tailored to their own organizational context.

modules.title: Case Studies: Real Zero-Click Exploits Amplified by Automation
modules.sub: Studying documented attacks reshaped by agentic automation
modules.description: Learners dissect real-world zero-click exploit cases and reason through how each would propagate further and faster if an AI agent were present in the affected pipeline. They extract repeatable attacker patterns from these cases and identify which architectural decisions turned a contained incident into an automated chain reaction.

modules.title: How Prompt Injection Becomes a Zero-Click Trigger
modules.sub: Turning adversarial text into silent, autonomous exploit chains
modules.description: Learners analyze how prompt injection payloads embedded in untrusted data sources can hijack agent instructions without any user awareness or action. They work through injection scenarios step by step, identifying the trust boundary failures that allow a crafted string to escalate into privilege abuse, data exfiltration, or lateral movement.

modules.title: Detection Strategies: Telemetry, Sandboxing, and Behavioral Baselines
modules.sub: Building visibility into what agents do and when they deviate
modules.description: Learners design a detection stack suited to agentic environments, selecting telemetry signals, sandbox configurations, and behavioral baseline thresholds that surface anomalous agent actions. They evaluate detection coverage gaps and practice triaging synthetic alert data to distinguish legitimate agent behavior from signs of zero-click compromise.

modules.title: Hardening Agentic Systems Against Unsolicited Input Exploitation
modules.sub: Reducing blast radius through architectural and policy controls
modules.description: Learners apply a layered hardening framework covering input validation, least-privilege permission scoping, tool call restrictions, and human-in-the-loop checkpoints for high-risk actions. They assess a reference agentic architecture for exploitable weaknesses and produce a prioritized remediation plan that limits how far a compromised agent can propagate damage.

modules.title: Red Team Lab: Simulating a Zero-Click Agent Compromise
modules.sub: Executing a controlled attack simulation to stress-test defenses
modules.description: Learners take on a red team role inside a sandboxed agentic environment, staging a prompt injection payload that triggers an automated exploit chain without any simulated user interaction. They document each stage of the kill chain, then switch perspective to evaluate which detective and preventive controls from earlier modules would have interrupted the attack and at what point.

synopsis: Zero-click attacks require no user action to execute — and as AI agents are granted access to email, files, and APIs, a single malicious input can silently chain through an entire organization's systems without anyone clicking anything. This course maps how attackers weaponize agentic architectures and equips security professionals to detect and contain these invisible intrusions.
rationale: The rapid enterprise adoption of AI agents with broad tool access has created a new class of zero-interaction attack surface that existing endpoint and perimeter defenses were never designed to cover.
learning_outcome: Learners can identify architectural conditions in agentic systems that enable zero-click exploitation, implement behavioral telemetry to detect anomalous agent actions, and apply hardening controls that limit blast radius when an agent is compromised.
source_signals: Zero-Click Attacks: AI Agents and the Next Cybersecurity Challenge
Comparing AI agents to cybersecurity professionals in real-world pen testing
